enrichment.short_description: Label Studio is an open-source, multi-modal data labeling and annotation platform for computer vision, NLP, audio, and LLM evaluation, built by HumanSignal.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (Community Edition, self-hosted); Starter Cloud from $99/user/month
enrichment.seo_title: Label Studio Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Label Studio is the leading open-source data labeling tool for AI and ML. See 2026 pricing, key features, HumanSignal Enterprise details, and top alternatives.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Label Studio is an open-source, multi-type data annotation and labeling platform used to prepare training data for machine learning models and to evaluate the outputs of AI systems, including large language models. The project was originally built under the name Heartex, founded in 2019 by Michael Malyuk and Max Tkachenko, and the company rebranded to HumanSignal in June 2023 to reflect its broader focus on tools for data science and AI teams working with foundation models. HumanSignal is headquartered in San Francisco, California, employs roughly 50 to 200 people distributed across several continents, and raised a $25 million Series A round in May 2022 led by Redpoint Ventures with participation from Unusual Ventures, Bow Capital, and Swift Ventures, bringing its total disclosed funding to about $30 million. Label Studio itself is described as the most widely used open-source data labeling tool, with more than one million AI practitioners reportedly using it and organizations such as Cloudflare, NVIDIA, Meta, IBM, and Intel cited among its users. Architecturally, Label Studio is a self-hostable web application with a configurable, XML-like labeling interface that administrators can adapt to nearly any annotation task without writing custom frontend code, plus an API, Python SDK, and webhook system that let engineering teams wire labeling directly into machine learning pipelines, connect external cloud storage as data sources, and plug in machine learning models to pre-label or actively assist annotators. The platform supports a wide range of data modalities within one tool, including computer vision tasks like image classification, object detection, and semantic segmentation; natural language processing tasks such as named entity recognition, sentiment analysis, and PDF or OCR-based document labeling; audio and speech tasks including transcription, speaker diarization, and emotion recognition; time-series data from IoT and sensor streams; and multi-modal projects that combine several of these data types in a single labeling interface. As LLM development has grown, Label Studio has added specialized tooling for reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), fine-tuning dataset collection, and building custom evaluation benchmarks and rubrics, including LLM-as-a-judge workflows available on the Enterprise tier. Commercially, the product follows a freemium structure with four effective tiers. The Community Edition is entirely free, open source, and self-hosted, giving technical teams full access to the core labeling engine, configurable interfaces, and API without licensing cost. Starter Cloud is a fully hosted plan priced at $99 per user per month for up to 12 users, offering a managed multi-modal labeling platform with role-based access control, quality workflows, automated task distribution, and a support portal, and it is available with a free trial. The Enterprise tier is custom-priced and adds everything in Starter plus single sign-on via SAML or LDAP, programmable and embeddable labeling interfaces, enhanced quality analytics, LLM-as-a-judge and bulk auto-labeling, SOC2 and HIPAA compliance for both cloud and on-premises deployments, the highest-priority SLAs, and a dedicated customer success manager. A separate Data Services offering provides project-based, custom-quoted human labeling services delivered through purpose-built facilities and domain experts, aimed at organizations that need outsourced annotation capacity rather than just software. Label Studio's core audience spans individual ML engineers and researchers who need a free, flexible labeling tool they can self-host, up through regulated enterprises and AI labs that need compliant, large-scale human-in-the-loop workflows for both traditional ML training data and modern LLM evaluation. Its most direct competitors include Labelbox, Scale AI, SuperAnnotate, Encord, V7 (Darwin), Kili Technology, Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth, and the open-source tool CVAT; Label Studio differentiates itself primarily through being genuinely open source at its core (unlike most rivals, which are closed-source SaaS products from the outset), its unusually broad support for many data modalities within a single unified interface rather than separate tools per data type, and its growing specialization in evaluating generative AI and LLM outputs alongside traditional annotation work.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Label Studio is an open-source data annotation platform built by HumanSignal (formerly Heartex) that lets teams label images, text, audio, video, and time-series data, as well as evaluate large language model outputs, all within one configurable interface.

The project is free and self-hostable at its core, with HumanSignal offering fully managed cloud and enterprise editions on top of the same open-source engine for teams that want hosting, compliance, and advanced quality-control features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Label Studio supports a wide range of labeling tasks through a configurable, XML-like interface, covering computer vision, NLP and document processing, audio and speech, time series, and multi-modal projects, all without requiring custom frontend development.

The platform integrates into ML pipelines via a REST API, Python SDK, and webhooks, supports machine-learning-assisted pre-labeling and active learning, and, on the Enterprise tier, adds LLM-as-a-judge evaluation, bulk auto-labeling, single sign-on, and detailed quality analytics.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Label Studio offers a free, open-source Community Edition for self-hosting, a hosted Starter Cloud plan at $99 per user per month for up to 12 users with a free trial, and a custom-priced Enterprise plan with SSO, SOC2/HIPAA compliance, and dedicated support.

A separate Data Services offering provides project-based, custom-quoted human labeling delivered by HumanSignal's own domain experts and facilities for organizations that need outsourced annotation capacity in addition to the software.

features.title: Multi-modal labeling interface
features.description: Configurable, XML-like UI supports images, video, audio, text, time series, and combined multi-modal annotation projects.

features.title: Programmable interfaces
features.description: Labeling layouts adapt to specific tasks and can be customized without writing frontend code.

features.title: API and Python SDK
features.description: Connects labeling workflows directly into ML pipelines, external data sources, and model backends.

features.title: ML-assisted and active learning
features.description: Connects prediction models to pre-label data and prioritize the most useful items for human review.

features.title: LLM evaluation tooling
features.description: Supports RLHF data collection, custom evaluation rubrics and benchmarks, and LLM-as-a-judge workflows.

features.title: Quality and review workflows
features.description: Enterprise tier adds reviewer assignment, quality analytics, and workspace management for large teams.

features.title: Self-hosted or cloud deployment
features.description: Runs free and self-hosted via the open-source Community Edition or fully managed through HumanSignal Cloud.

features.title: Enterprise security and compliance
features.description: SSO via SAML/LDAP, role-based access control, and SOC2/HIPAA compliance for regulated deployments.

pros: Genuinely open-source core with no functional limitations for self-hosted use
Supports an unusually broad range of data types in a single unified tool
Strong ML pipeline integration through a documented API and Python SDK
Actively used and trusted by major technology companies for both classic ML and LLM evaluation
Growing feature set specifically aimed at LLM and generative AI evaluation, not just static labeling
cons: Self-hosting requires infrastructure and DevOps knowledge that non-technical teams may lack
Advanced quality, SSO, and compliance features are locked behind the paid Enterprise tier
Starter Cloud pricing is per-user, which can get expensive for larger labeling teams
Enterprise and Data Services pricing is custom and not published, requiring a sales conversation
UI configuration syntax has a learning curve for highly customized labeling tasks

faqs.question: Is Label Studio free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition is free and open source for self-hosted use. Paid Starter Cloud and Enterprise tiers add hosting, support, and advanced compliance and quality-control features.

faqs.question: Who owns Label Studio?
faqs.answer: Label Studio is developed by HumanSignal, a company originally founded as Heartex in 2019 by Michael Malyuk and Max Tkachenko, which rebranded to HumanSignal in 2023.

faqs.question: Can Label Studio be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, the open-source Community Edition can be self-hosted on a team's own infrastructure at no licensing cost, and the Enterprise tier also supports on-premises deployment.

faqs.question: What data types can Label Studio annotate?
faqs.answer: Label Studio supports images, video, audio, text, PDFs, time-series data, and multi-modal combinations within a single configurable labeling interface.

faqs.question: Does Label Studio support LLM evaluation?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes tooling for RLHF data collection, custom evaluation rubrics and benchmarks, and LLM-as-a-judge workflows, primarily on the Enterprise tier.

faqs.question: What are the main alternatives to Label Studio?
faqs.answer: Common alternatives include Labelbox, Scale AI, SuperAnnotate, Encord, V7, Kili Technology, and the open-source tool CVAT.

use_cases.title: LLM and generative AI evaluation
